adjudication_raw:
Review: Music retrieval model evaluation.

Verdict: critique_wins

Three compounding issues prevent the Recall@100 gap from supporting the paper's claim of neural retrieval quality: (1) supervision source mismatch — impression data with negatives vs. positive-only play events; (2) feature set mismatch — audio embeddings + skip behavior vs. play events only; (3) no ablation baseline to isolate architecture contribution. The paper acknowledges the supervision asymmetry by noting the cost of reconstruction but does not adjust the comparison. The internal reviewers' question is exactly right.
